title: Algorithms to Live By
subtitle: The Computer Science of Human Decisions
author: Brian Christian, Tom Griffiths
themes: The explore/exploit tradeoff across life stages
Algorithmic thinking as a decision-making framework
Tradeoffs between time, accuracy, and resources
awards: MIT Technology Review Best Books of 2016
excerpt: What should we do, and leave undone, in a day or in a decade? What degree of mess should we embrace? What balance between new experiences and favored ones makes for the most fulfilling life? Computer scientists have been grappling with these same dilemmas — and their solutions offer a surprisingly practical guide to human decisions.
